children.heading: Listing standards
children.label: 1 Listing standards
children.text: The Commission shall, by rule, direct the national securities exchanges and national securities associations to prohibit the listing of any equity security of an issuer, other than an issuer that is a controlled company, limited partnership, company in bankruptcy proceedings, open-ended management investment company that is registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 [ et seq.], or a foreign private issuer that provides annual disclosures to shareholders of the reasons that the foreign private issuer does not have an independent compensation committee, that does not comply with the requirements of this subsection.15 U.S.C. 80a–1

children.label: A
children.text: a member of the board of directors of the issuer; and

children.label: B
children.text: independent.

children.label: A
children.text: the source of compensation of a member of the board of directors of an issuer, including any consulting, advisory, or other compensatory fee paid by the issuer to such member of the board of directors; and

children.label: B
children.text: whether a member of the board of directors of an issuer is affiliated with the issuer, a subsidiary of the issuer, or an affiliate of a subsidiary of the issuer.

children.heading: Exemption authority
children.label: 4 Exemption authority
children.text: The rules of the Commission under paragraph (1) shall permit a national securities exchange or a national securities association to exempt a particular relationship from the requirements of paragraph (2), with respect to the members of a compensation committee, as the national securities exchange or national securities association determines is appropriate, taking into consideration the size of an issuer and any other relevant factors.

children.heading: Independence of compensation consultants and other compensation committee advisers
children.label: b Independence of compensation consultants and other compensation committee advisers

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The compensation committee of an issuer may only select a compensation consultant, legal counsel, or other adviser to the compensation committee after taking into consideration the factors identified by the Commission under paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: the provision of other services to the issuer by the person that employs the compensation consultant, legal counsel, or other adviser;

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of fees received from the issuer by the person that employs the compensation consultant, legal counsel, or other adviser, as a percentage of the total revenue of the person that employs the compensation consultant, legal counsel, or other adviser;

children.label: C
children.text: the policies and procedures of the person that employs the compensation consultant, legal counsel, or other adviser that are designed to prevent conflicts of interest;

children.label: D
children.text: any business or personal relationship of the compensation consultant, legal counsel, or other adviser with a member of the compensation committee; and

children.label: E
children.text: any stock of the issuer owned by the compensation consultant, legal counsel, or other adviser.

children.heading: Compensation committee authority relating to compensation consultants
children.label: c Compensation committee authority relating to compensation consultants

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The compensation committee of an issuer, in its capacity as a committee of the board of directors, may, in its sole discretion, retain or obtain the advice of a compensation consultant.

children.heading: Direct responsibility of compensation committee
children.label: B Direct responsibility of compensation committee
children.text: The compensation committee of an issuer shall be directly responsible for the appointment, compensation, and oversight of the work of a compensation consultant.

children.label: i
children.text: to require the compensation committee to implement or act consistently with the advice or recommendations of the compensation consultant; or

children.label: ii
children.text: to affect the ability or obligation of a compensation committee to exercise its own judgment in fulfillment of the duties of the compensation committee.

children.label: A
children.text: the compensation committee of the issuer retained or obtained the advice of a compensation consultant; and

children.label: B
children.text: the work of the compensation consultant has raised any conflict of interest and, if so, the nature of the conflict and how the conflict is being addressed.

children.heading: Authority to engage independent legal counsel and other advisers
children.label: d Authority to engage independent legal counsel and other advisers

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The compensation committee of an issuer, in its capacity as a committee of the board of directors, may, in its sole discretion, retain and obtain the advice of independent legal counsel and other advisers.

children.heading: Direct responsibility of compensation committee
children.label: 2 Direct responsibility of compensation committee
children.text: The compensation committee of an issuer shall be directly responsible for the appointment, compensation, and oversight of the work of independent legal counsel and other advisers.

children.label: A
children.text: to require a compensation committee to implement or act consistently with the advice or recommendations of independent legal counsel or other advisers under this subsection; or

children.label: B
children.text: to affect the ability or obligation of a compensation committee to exercise its own judgment in fulfillment of the duties of the compensation committee.

children.heading: Compensation of compensation consultants, independent legal counsel, and other advisers
children.label: e Compensation of compensation consultants, independent legal counsel, and other advisers

children.text: to a compensation consultant; and
to independent legal counsel or any other adviser to the compensation committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 360 days after , the Commission shall, by rule, direct the national securities exchanges and national securities associations to prohibit the listing of any security of an issuer that is not in compliance with the requirements of this section.July 21, 2010

children.heading: Opportunity to cure defects
children.label: 2 Opportunity to cure defects
children.text: The rules of the Commission under paragraph (1) shall provide for appropriate procedures for an issuer to have a reasonable opportunity to cure any defects that would be the basis for the prohibition under paragraph (1), before the imposition of such prohibition.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The rules of the Commission under paragraph (1) shall permit a national securities exchange or a national securities association to exempt a category of issuers from the requirements under this section, as the national securities exchange or the national securities association determines is appropriate.

children.heading: Considerations
children.label: B Considerations
children.text: In determining appropriate exemptions under subparagraph (A), the national securities exchange or the national securities association shall take into account the potential impact of the requirements of this section on smaller reporting issuers.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: This section shall not apply to any controlled company.

children.label: A
children.text: that is listed on a national securities exchange or by a national securities association; and

children.label: B
children.text: that holds an election for the board of directors of the issuer in which more than 50 percent of the voting power is held by an individual, a group, or another issuer.
